mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 5% signals tightening labor market conditions that boost corporate earnings visibility, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE declines below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in GDP (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates strong aggregate demand, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days through increased corporate revenue expectations.
failure_condition: Joint growth in GDPC1 and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.4.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and inflation (CPIAUCSL) signal a tightening monetary environment that raises equity discount rates, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and CPIAUCSL > 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects stronger manufacturing activity, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days through improved corporate earnings momentum.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain negative when INDPRO increases by > 1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 2.5% (CPIAUCSL) prompts central bank rate hikes, which increase borrowing costs and reduce equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in payrolls (PAYEMS) and declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a robust labor market that boosts consumer spending, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth in PAYEMS and decline in UNRATE fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 50% of cases, or their correlation drops below 0.3.

mechanism_narrative: High real sales (RSAFS) combined with slowing GDP growth (GDPC1) indicates overleveraged consumers in a late-cycle economy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of instances where RSAFS exceeds 600,000 and GDPC1 growth is below 2%.
